rulings.Case Title: AARON HUGHES VS. MEDICAL HAIR RESTORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings-Improper Forum, Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 17, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT LEAVITT MEDICAL ASSOCIATES OF CALIFORNIA, M.H.R. MEDICAL ASSOCIATES OF CALIFORNIA, BRADLEY KURGIS, D.O. AND LEAVITT MANAGEMENT GROUP'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings-Improper Forum (Request For Judicial Notice) IS DENIED. DEFENDANT WAIVED THE RIGHT TO SEEK TO ENFORCE THE CLAUSE BY NOT ASSERTING IT WHEN THE TORT CLAIM WAS FILED. THE FACTS ALSO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR A FINDING OF EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL WITH RESPECT TO THE CONSENT TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: BRIGHT NOW! DENTAL, INC., A WASHINGTON VS. TERRY OLSON et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO STANISLAUS COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike Pursuant To Anti Slapp
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 17, 2007, LINE 7. DEFENDANT TERRY OLSON'S Special MOTION To Strike Pursuant To Anti-SLAPP IS DENIED. DEFENDANT CANNOT MEET THE PUBLIC INTEREST REQUIREMENT. DUCHARME V INT'L, ETC. (2003) 110 CAL APP 4TH 107, 119; KURWA V HARRINGTON, ET AL. (2007) CAL APP 4TH 841. SECOND, THIS IS A COMMERCIAL DISPUTE AND THE RECORD ESTABLISHES THAT OLSEN HAS SIGNIFICANT INVOLVEMENT IN THE PELANDALE BUSINESS. THIRD, THE RECORD FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT OLSEN'S ALLEGED CONDUCT IS PROTECTED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER GOVEA VS. UNITED STATES PIPE AND FOUNDRY COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 17, 2007, LINE 15. DEFENDANT UNITED STATES PIPE AND FOUNDRY COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. There is an agreement between the parties to arbitrate based upon plaintiff remaining on the job after the receipt of the dispute resolution program. Craig v. Brown & Root Inc. (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 416. The agreement is one of adhesion given the manner in which it was imposed and plaintiff's inability to effect any of the terms. The agreement is contrary to the mandate of Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. It is both procedurally and substantively unconscionable. The agreement precludes the arbitrator from "substitut[ing] his/her judgment for the Company rules, practices and performance. The arbitrator must enforce, and cannot deviate in any way from, these rules and procedures." No judicial officer would ever be subject to such a restraint. The agreement also carves out certain claims. An analysis of those claims demonstrates a lack of mutuality. Certain of the carved out claims are subject to mandatory statutory requirements that effectively preclude arbitration - unemployment compensation, workplace safety, pension and benefit plans, criminal charges and labor relations. At the same time, the agreement gives the employer the right to file suit and avoid arbitration for the most likely claims an employer would have against an employee. In contrast, the most likely employee claims require arbitration. The potential discovery limitations standing alone are not procedurally unconscionable. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 17, 2007, LINE 8. DEFENDANT THELEN REID, ET AL.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. rulings.Case Title: LUXIM CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. JONES DAY, AN OHIO PARTNERSHIP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Jones Day
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, SEPTEMBER 17, 2007, LINE 12. PLAINTIFF LUXIM CORPORATION'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction Against Jones Day: MATTER IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 10, 2007 AT 9:30 A.M.. THE PARTIES ARE TO PREPARE A SINGLE DOCUMENT THAT REFLECTS THE MOVING PARTY'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE AND THE OPPOSITION OF THE PROPONENT OF THE EVIDENCE. EACH OBJECTION AND OPPOSITION SHALL BE ON A SINGLE PAGE. THE TEXT IS TO BE DOUBLE SPACED, AND DIVIDED EQUALLY BETWEEN THE PARTIES USING LINES 1 THROUGH 24. LINES 25 TO 28 SHALL INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING: ON LINE 25, SET FORTH A LINE FOR THE COURT TO CHECK "GRANTED" OR "DENIED" OR "GRANTED IN PART/DENIED IN PART". LINES 27 AND 28 ARE TO CONTAIN A SINGLE LINE ON EACH FOR THE THE COURT TO EXPLAIN, IF NECESSARY, THE RULING. THIS DOCUMENT SHALL BE FILED WITH THE COURT ON OCTOBER 2. 2007. =(302/PJM)
